name: Happy Cat Public Storefront Host

securitySheaf.label: Probe IC
securitySheaf.effect: Happy Cat starts checking the intruder icon and access path.

securitySheaf.label: Trace IC
securitySheaf.effect: The storefront host attempts to establish or accelerate a trace.

securitySheaf.label: Scramble IC
securitySheaf.effect: Store records and receipt trails begin to shuffle under coupon-noise interference.

securitySheaf.label: Store-brand Tar Baby
securitySheaf.effect: Nuisance IC attempts to hold the icon in place for follow-up.

securitySheaf.label: Pixel Sticks Tripwire
securitySheaf.effect: The hidden layer notices a non-local icon sniffing around the handoff seam.

securitySheaf.label: Hidden Host Trace Burst
securitySheaf.effect: The deeper host treats the intrusion as hostile and begins burning the route back toward the decker.

sculpting: Bright retail storefront, mascot popups, collectible-card catalog, snack ads, register hooks, employee stockroom doors, and local teen chatter overlays. Under a misprinted receipt/SKU pattern sits a concealed handoff into a much harder Pixel Sticks stash host.
notes: Starter host refactored around the public-door/secure-door template. The public side is harmless flavor. The secure side can reveal mundane store records, a strange pet-food order clue, and a hidden Target: Matrix-style deeper host used by Pixel Sticks.

nodes.title: Local Grid Outside Happy Cat
nodes.description: You hover in the east Nashville grid among cheap public icons, snack ads, apartment-block chatter, and the glowing storefront sign for Happy Cat. The host presents a customer-facing public door and a staff-only private surface.

choices.label: Break into the staff-only host surface

nodes.title: Mascot Storefront
nodes.description: A lucky-cat mascot bows from behind a neon counter. This is the official visitor side: cute, noisy, and almost aggressively harmless.

choices.label: Browse cards, snacks, and public specials
Check event flyers and teen hangout notices

nodes.title: Public Catalog Aisles
nodes.description: Trading-card binders, snack specials, novelty charms, and public inventory blurbs spin under cheerful sale banners. No private records live here.

choices.label: Make a note: public catalog confirms ordinary retail traffic

nodes.title: Event Flyer Corkboard
nodes.description: The public board lists trading nights, snack promos, and local customer chatter. Tell the GM if you want to use this as cover for ordinary Happy Cat foot traffic.

nodes.title: Customer Help Kiosk
nodes.description: A bubbly help icon offers store hours, return policy, loyalty signup, and a chirpy apology for any missing snack inventory. Nothing here should reveal the run's real secret.

nodes.title: Staff-Only Access Pinpad
nodes.description: The public mascot skin falls away just enough to show a staff pinpad, a stockroom icon, and routine small-business permissions. Failure here should reveal nothing more about the private host.

nodes.title: Staff Subsystem Hub
nodes.description: You are inside the private side of the Happy Cat host. Store files, register hooks, camera pointers, and vendor records are available if you can keep the tally under control.

nodes.title: Customer Files Access
nodes.description: You appear to have access to customer loyalty files and small purchase histories. Tell the GM you have customer-file access; ask what names, handles, dates, or purchase categories can be searched.

choices.label: Search for repeat teen handles and hangout timing
Compare customer patterns against missing vandal tags

nodes.title: Shipping Records Access
nodes.description: You appear to have access to shipping records, supplier invoices, delivery windows, and restock queues. Tell the GM you have shipping-record access; ask what vendors, dates, and item classes are visible.

choices.label: Pull mundane supplier and delivery-window notes
Compare invoice checksums against receipt noise

nodes.title: Camera Network Access
nodes.description: You appear to have access to Happy Cat camera pointers. Tell the GM you have camera-network access; confirm which feeds are live, stored, missing, delayed, or spoofed.

choices.label: Make a note: camera network access exists
Search camera timestamps for missing-tag gaps

nodes.title: Register and Stock Controls
nodes.description: You appear to have access to register queues, shelf displays, and stock-control hooks. Tell the GM you have register/stock access; confirm whether this is live control, records-only access, or delayed command access.

choices.label: Copy register timing and stock discrepancy notes

nodes.title: Mundane Useful Leads Cache
nodes.description: Make a note: Happy Cat contains mundane but useful retail paydata, including customer loyalty crumbs, delivery windows, supplier names, camera timestamps, and register/stock discrepancies. Tell the GM which category you accessed for exact details.

nodes.title: Strange Pet-Food Orders
nodes.description: You find records of lots of strange pet food being ordered through or near Happy Cat's supply flow. Tell the GM you found the strange pet-food order records and ask for further details here. Make a personal note: pet-food order anomaly exists in Happy Cat shipping records.

choices.label: Compare pet-food invoices against receipt checksum noise

nodes.title: Misprinted Receipt / SKU Anomaly
nodes.description: The records do not say Pixel Sticks. They do not say secret host. They show misprinted SKUs, receipt timing jitter, and a little knot of data that behaves like a bookmark hidden in ordinary retail noise.

choices.label: Package the anomaly as a clue and back out

nodes.title: Unlisted LTG Handshake
nodes.description: Surprise: the receipt noise resolves into a hidden handoff, not a store record. Something deeper is using Happy Cat's ordinary traffic as cover. The next door is much harder than the storefront host.

choices.label: Breach the concealed Pixel Sticks host

nodes.title: Pixel Sticks Hidden Tag Yard
nodes.description: The cheerful store sculpting is gone. You are in a harder, cleaner, graffiti-coded staging host: tag fragments, utility crates, dead-drop lockers, and route maps hang in a digital underpass.

nodes.title: Pixel Sticks Future Plans
nodes.description: You recover a protected future-plans cache from the hidden host. Tell the GM you cracked the Pixel Sticks future-plans reward; the GM will provide the actual plan details.

choices.label: Return to the hidden tag yard

nodes.title: Pixel Sticks Member List
nodes.description: You recover a protected member-list cache. Tell the GM you cracked the Pixel Sticks member-list reward; the GM will provide the roster if this recovery stands.

choices.label: Return to the hidden tag yard

nodes.title: Pixel Sticks Paydata Stash
nodes.description: You recover a stash of paydata hidden by the Pixel Sticks. Tell the GM you cracked the paydata-stash reward; value and fences will need to be determined after recovery.

choices.label: Return to the hidden tag yard

nodes.title: Clean Exit
nodes.description: The storefront folds back into neon signs and public chatter. If the tally stayed low, nobody knows the mascot let you look behind the counter—or what else was hiding under the receipts.
